content:
📜 Why This Works:
✔ Gives the professor active influence beyond vetoing.
✔ Allows different playstyles—some weeks are structured, others chaotic.
✔ Ties into literary archetypes of law, philosophy, trickery, and absurdism.

📜 2. The Faustian Bargain – Trading Power for Peril

📜 Once per semester, a student may be offered a Faustian Bargain—trading something valuable for a hidden cost.

✔ The offer is made randomly in class, in secret (only the chosen student knows).
✔ The deal always grants immediate power but with an unknown consequence.
✔ The student must accept or decline before knowing the cost.

📖 Example Faustian Bargains
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 371 (chunk 371/555)
